steps: Go to https://dashboard.balena-cloud.com and log in to your Balena account
Click on your avatar or account menu in the upper-right corner
Give the token a name and optionally set an expiry
Copy the generated token — it is shown only once
Use the token as a Bearer token in the Authorization header

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Token
credential_fields.description: Your Balena Cloud API token, sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'

notes: Two token types exist: session tokens (expire after 7 days, cannot be revoked) and API keys (persistent, can be revoked)
Use API keys for automation and agents — session tokens are for interactive use
API keys provide full user-level permissions across all devices and fleets
Free tier available for up to 10 devices
The base URL is https://api.balena-cloud.com (not api.balena.io) for production

test_endpoint.description: List all applications (fleets) to verify your token is valid
